title: Particles including polytetrafluoroethylene and process for making a three-dimensional article
abstract: Particles of a polytetrafluoroethylene for use in additive manufacturing. The polytetrafluoroethylene includes first polymerized units of tetrafluoroethylene and second polymerized units of at least one of a fluorinated vinyl ether or a fluorinated allyl ether. The second polymerized units are present in an amount not more than one percent by weight based on the total weight of the first polymerized units and the second polymerized units. The particles have a melt flow index of at least 0.5 gram/10 minutes (372 ËC/5 kg) and a melting point of at least 316 ËC, measured after the first melting and crystallization of the particles. The polytetrafluoroethylene may have a core and a shell, in which the shell has a greater amount of the second polymerized units than the core. A process for making a three-dimensional article using the particles, a three-dimensional article made by the process, and use of the particles for selective laser sintering are also disclosed.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. Particles comprising a polytetrafluoroethylene for additive manufacturing, the polytetrafluoroethylene comprising first polymerized units of tetrafluoroethylene and second polymerized units of at least one of a fluorinated vinyl ether or a fluorinated allyl ether, wherein the second polymerized units are present in an amount not more than one percent by weight based on the total weight of the first polymerized units and the second polymerized units, the particles having a melt flow index of at least 0.5 gram/10 minutes (372 Â°C/5 kg) and a melting point of at least 316 Â°C, measured after the first melting and crystallization of the particles. 2. Particles of claim 1, wherein the second polymerized units are independently represented by formula wherein Rf is a linear or branched perfluoroalkyl group having from 1 to 8 carbon atoms and optionally interrupted by one or more -O- groups, z is 0, 1, or 2, each n is independently 1, 2, 3, or 4, and m is 0 or 1. 3. Particles of claim 2, wherein at least one of z is 1 or 2, or Rf is interrupted by one or more -O- groups. 4. Particles of claim 2 or 3, where the second polymerized units comprise polymerized units of the fluorinated allyl ether, and wherein m is 1. 5. Particles of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the fluorinated vinyl ether or the fluorinated allyl ether is perfluoromethyl vinyl ether, perfluoropropyl vinyl ether, perfluoro-3-methoxy-n-propyl vinyl ether, perfluoro-3-methoxy-n-propyl allyl ether, perfluoromethyl allyl ether, perfluoropropyl allyl ether, or a combination thereof. 6. Particles of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the polytetrafluoroethylene has a core and a shell, wherein the shell has a greater amount of the second polymerized units than the core. 7. Particles of claim 6, wherein the shell comprises the second polymerized units in not more than five percent by weight, based on the total weight of the first polymerized units and the second polymerized units, and wherein the core comprises the second polymerized units in not more than 0.2 percent by weight, based on the total weight of the first polymerized units and the second polymerized units. 8. Particles of any one of claims 1 to 7, having a melt flow index of at least 1 gram/10 minutes (372 Â°C/5 kg). 9. Particles of any one of claims 1 to 8, having a melting point of at least 320 Â°C, measured after the first melting and crystallization of the particles. 10. Particles of any one of claims 1 to 9, having a particle size (dsn) in a range from 1 micrometer to 100 micrometers. 11. A process for making a three-dimensional article, the process comprising subjecting the particles of any one of claims 1 to 10 to additive manufacturing. 12. A process for making a three-dimensional article, the process comprising: providing a layer of the particles of any one of claims 1 to 10; and selectively treating an area of the layer of the particles by irradiation with a laser beam to fuse at least some of the particles. 13. The process of claim 11 or 12, further comprising: retrieving, from a non-transitory machine readable medium, data representing a model of the three-dimensional article; executing, by one or more processors interfacing with a manufacturing device, manufacturing instructions using the data; and generating, by the manufacturing device, the three-dimensional article. 14. A three-dimensional article prepared by the process of any one of claims 11 to 13. 15. Use of the particles of any one of claims 1 to 10 for selective laser sintering.
